projects.title: Intuit
description: A D library for talking to language and embedding model endpoints through native D structures instead of thin JSON shims, currently covering both OpenAI-compatible APIs (including local LMStudio-style servers, Qwen, Deepseek, Phi) and Claude.

Provides chat completions with streaming, single and batch embeddings, tool usage, and per-model configuration of temperature, max tokens, top-p, and presence/frequency penalties via a persistent Model object.

Ships SIMD-optimized vector operations in `intuit.space` (cosine similarity, dot product, Euclidean distance, L2 norm, in-place normalization, normalized mean) using AVX intrinsics, and leans on D's compile-time metaprogramming for type-safe JSON serialization.

projects.title: Akashi
description: Corpus for PubChem, Wikipedia, Psychonaut Wiki, PubMed, and PMC — a D library for chemistry-aware retrieval, source resolution, and text parsing across public scientific and knowledge sources.

Unifies these behind a shared `Page` type that lazily fetches content and exposes `raw`, `document`, `sections`, `preamble`, and `fulltext`, with `akashi.text` parsing wikitext and XML into a compact AST for rendering or plain-text extraction.

Includes cross-source resolution (e.g. resolving a PubChem compound to its Wikipedia or PsychonautWiki page), similarity search on PubChem CIDs, and reusable HTTP + rate-limiting primitives via the `conductor` library for building new source adapters. Designed to pair with Intuit but fully standalone.

projects.title: Chemica
description: A GTK-based PubChem wiki app for compounds with a 3D molecular view (small and expanded) featuring atomic tooltips and full bond visualization, alongside chemical properties (XLogP, MW, formula, charge, energy) and identifiers (SMILES, InChI, InChIKey, CAS).

Automatically aggregates dosage data (route of administration, bioavailability, thresholds) and performs similarity matching using structural scoring combined with XLogP and MW to locate analogous compounds and fill gaps in dosage information.

Builds on Akashi to pull in Wikipedia, PsychonautWiki/Erowid, PubMed, and PMC, with an `infer` layer (`ease`, `config`, `resolve`) that uses local AI synthesis to blend overlapping sources into coherent entries.

projects.title: Multidasm
projects.description: Dynamic assembler built from the ground up for D comptime integration.

projects.title: Webull-SDK
description: Webull SDK for D with native-feeling finance types: typed market data (OHLCV, snapshots, L2 depth, ticks, footprint) and account access across US/HK/CN equities, options, crypto, and futures.
Handles full HMAC-SHA1 request signing, MFA token flows, and session refresh so integrations stay authenticated without manual intervention.
Designed for algorithmic strategies and dashboards that need real-time data without wrestling the wire format directly.

projects.title: Jitsort
projects.description: Just-In-Time sorting algorithm that dynamically generates code to sort data in O(1)!

projects.title: AutoHandshake!
description: A Ruby + JavaScript workflow automation suite for Handshake AI that replaces repetitive manual annotation and triage work with scripted, auditable runs designed to support contractors at scale.

Uses Selenium alongside injected JavaScript hooks to monitor page state, coordinate multi-tab workflows, recover from UI stalls, and keep long-running sessions reliable even as the interface shifts underneath it.
Parses HTML for workflow context and quality-of-life helpers, giving workers fine-grained control while still cutting manual effort by up to 95%.

projects.title: LinkedIn
projects.description: CLI and library for interacting with LinkedIn Learning and aggregating course and path data using Selenium and Nokogiri.

projects.title: Nimbus
description: Wayland tool for unified display configuration — a lightweight Linux CLI for controlling display brightness, contrast, gamma, color temperature, and arbitrary VCP features.

Supports human-friendly labels (`light`, `brightness`, `backlight`, `contrast`, `gamma`, `temperature`) as well as raw hex VCP codes, with percentage or absolute values and multi-value commands like `nimbus set light=80%, contrast=60`.

Auto-detects connected displays and their capabilities (`nimbus list`), prefers backlight control over DDC brightness when both are available, and supports range queries such as `nimbus get min/light/max` for scripting use.

projects.title: Mobster
projects.description: Alternative to Lobster (lobster.sh) for watching movies, shows, etc. from the terminal with support for cover art and quality. Implementation of the BitTorrent protocol in D as well as Python streaming support for torrent providers.

projects.title: Intel Intrinsics
projects.description: The Dlang SIMD library — fork of intel-intrinsics adding AVX-512 and a dozen-plus additional intrinsics; merged upstream.

projects.title: Cassowary
description: Managed CoreCLR exposure and reflection library inspired by Novus — a managed-C# library that exposes CoreCLR internals directly, positioned as an alternative to Son of Strike for code that needs to reach past the BCL into the runtime itself.

Models the core CLR structures as first-class C# types: `MethodTable`, `EEClass` (including `ArrayClass`, `DelegateEEClass`, `LayoutEEClass`), `MethodDesc` / `MethodDescChunk`, `FieldDesc`, `Stub`, `UMThunkMarshInfo`, `NativeCodeVersion`, `WriteableData`, `CCWTemplate`, `GuidInfo`, `SparseVTableMap`, and related metadata.

Enables reflection-like operations on top of those structures: object allocation, instance construction, initialization, casts, boxing, and VTable/layout inspection, without dropping into native code.
projects.note: No longer maintained; succeeded by Godwit.

projects.title: Godwit
projects.description: Comprehensive reverse analysis and exposure of CoreCLR internals from D. Successor to Cassowary: all CLR structures modeled, serving as a reference implementation while the behavior layer is ported over.

projects.title: Tern
projects.description: Comprehensive and efficient standard library extension for D's Phobos, specializing in compile-time tooling and object manipulation. Extensive standard library expansion focusing on performance and comptime features.
projects.note: The project is maintained, but current effort is focused on a rewrite tailored to newer needs.

projects.title: Pipit
projects.description: Pipit is an exceedingly fast terminal text editor designed for simplicity, performance, and tight constraints. Blazingly fast terminal editor leveraging AVX2 and AVX-512.

projects.title: Depcat
projects.description: TModLoader (Terraria) mod for dynamic dependency injection of unmanaged code.

projects.title: Swaypaper
projects.description: Minimal SWWW wrapper for slideshow wallpapers from multiple directories.

projects.title: Challenge 02
projects.description: Challenge library for SIMD high entropy encryption algorithms, CAHS 2024.

projects.title: GarbageCorruptor
projects.description: Corrupts the GC heap as optimally as possible. Experiment on CoreCLR exposure.

projects.title: UndeadInterop
projects.description: Syscall library for C#, using shellcode and exports to resolve and execute syscalls as well as detect hooks on functions.

projects.title: Rio
projects.description: Assembly-like dynamic scripting language for C#. Currently not being worked on, since Cassowary needs to be completed first.

projects.title: MihoyoEXP
projects.description: Obsolete Mhyprot2 exploitation library capable of advanced process and memory manipulation with vulnerable IOCTL communications.

projects.title: VEXAPI
projects.description: Reverse analysis and documentation of VEX Robotics internal firmware and APIs.

projects.title: Mutagen
projects.description: Format support for Flac, Mp4, and Mp3 as well as full catalog parsing for tracks, albums, and artists.

projects.title: Git-Around
projects.description: Configurable Python Git housekeeping utility for maintaining local repositories and modules.

projects.title: Sim-D
projects.description: D SIMD implementation using static arrays for near-native vectors.

projects.title: Insurgent
projects.description: Insurgent is a companion client for Supremacy WW3 which runs alongside the game in Chrome. It supports a dashboard which allows for quick-lookup of unit stacks, a full catalog containing in-depth data on all units, research, and buildings, and live match data on all nations and stacks visible on the map. All data is collected live and aims to be minimally invasive and lightweight.
projects.note: Under the purview of Twin Harbor

projects.title: Nosferatu
projects.description: OOP-native, efficient HTML and JavaScript parser and GDI renderer.

projects.title: SMBIOSReader
projects.description: Fully featured SMBIOS reader and DSP interpreter.

projects.title: Google-SDK
description: Google Drive API with support for OAuth, accounts, and full file system with metadata — a D library for Google Drive with pragmatic Docs and Sheets text export helpers.

Exposes a Drive-first API (`Session`, `Identity`, `Folder`, `File`, `IFile`) covering OAuth login, account identity, folder and file listing, metadata refresh, upload, download, save, and delete, with a shared `IFile` surface unifying metadata plus `read`, `write`, `save`, and `text`.

Adds focused `google.docs` and `google.sheets` modules for MIME detection and lightweight plain-text / CSV export from Google-native files, with `File.text` dispatching to them automatically. Built on `conductor` for OAuth and HTTP primitives.

projects.title: Conductor
description: HTTP, OAuth, serialization, and network orchestration utilities for API integrations, designed to remove boilerplate when wiring D applications to external services.
Covers request building with typed parameter maps, token refresh flows, JSON and form serialization, connection pooling, and retry/circuit helpers.
Powers the transport layer for Intuit, Akashi, Google-SDK, and Warbler.

projects.title: Warbler
description: A D CLI interface backend for academic institutions, providing a git-esque command structure for grading papers, generating rubrics, and organizing classrooms through configurable endpoints.

Defines an institutional plugin model that maps commands like `warbler grade`, `warbler rubric`, and `warbler roster` to arbitrary LMS backends, with per-school configuration files and OAuth-based authentication.
Built on Conductor for HTTP and OAuth primitives, designed to be scriptable, auditable, and composable into larger academic workflows.

projects.title: Selenium-SDK
projects.description: Modern Selenium SDK for D, inspired by Ruby's Selenium — provides a clean, typed interface for browser automation, element queries, and session management.

projects.title: Cilk
projects.description: CIL disassembler and assembler for D — reads and writes Common Intermediate Language bytecode with a focus on comptime integration and low-allocation parsing.

projects.title: Head
projects.description: PE and ELF format support for D — parses executables, object files, and core dumps into structured types suitable for analysis and manipulation.

projects.title: Regex
projects.description: D regex library experimenting with alternative matching engines and compile-time pattern compilation for zero-runtime-overhead text scanning.

projects.title: Soundcore A3954 Dump
projects.description: Reverse-engineered state packets for the Soundcore Liberty 4 Pro (Model A3954). Raw BLE protocol captures decoded into structured packet definitions for downstream tooling.
projects.note: Archived; served as the reference for OpenSCQ30 integration.

projects.title: OpenSCQ30
description: Cross platform application for controlling settings of Soundcore headphones, earbuds, and speakers. Supports desktop (CLI and GUI) and Android.

Implements the Soundcore Liberty 4 Pro (Model A3954) configuration protocol on top of the OpenSCQ30 open-source framework, exposing EQ profiles, ANC modes, and firmware metadata through a unified interface.
Built on the reverse-engineered packet definitions from soundcore-a3954-dump, bridging raw BLE captures to a usable cross-platform configuration tool.
